records.ety: [OE. propre, F. propre, fr. L. proprius. Cf. Appropriate.]

defs: Belonging to one; one's own; individual.
quotes: Now learn the difference, at your proper cost, Betwixt true valor and an empty boast.

defs: Belonging to the natural or essential constitution; peculiar; not common; particular; as, every animal has his proper instincts and appetites.
quotes: Those high and peculiar attributes . . . which constitute our proper humanity.

defs: Befitting one's nature, qualities, etc.; suitable in all respect; appropriate; right; fit; decent; as, water is the proper element for fish; a proper dress.
quotes: The proper study of mankind is man.
In Athens all was pleasure, mirth, and play, All proper to the spring, and sprightly May.

defs: Becoming in appearance; well formed; handsome.
quotes: Moses . . . was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child.

defs: Pertaining to one of a species, but not common to the whole; not appellative; -- opposed to common; as, a proper name; Dublin is the proper name of a city.
Rightly so called; strictly considered; as, Greece proper; the garden proper.
Represented in its natural color; -- said of any object used as a charge.
Properly; hence, to a great degree; very; as, proper good.
